Field Label:
Connecting Researchers • Building Collaborations • Accelerating Discovery
 
 
 

🎯 What is ECRIN?

 

ECRIN (Enhanced Collaborative Research Insight Network) is an innovative digital platform designed to revolutionize scientific collaboration across coastal, maritime, and island territories. We connect researchers from the Campus Polytechnique des Territoires Maritimes et Portuaires (Campus PTMP) and the EUNICoast European Alliance.

Our mission is to break down silos between institutions, facilitate knowledge sharing, and create unprecedented opportunities for interdisciplinary research collaboration.

 

🌊 Our Focus Areas

 

ECRIN concentrates on five strategic research hubs that define the future of coastal and maritime territories:

History & Cultural Heritage - Preserving maritime legacy

Blue Economy & Logistics - Sustainable maritime economics

Governance & Planning - Smart territorial development

Biodiversity & Marine Sciences - Ocean ecosystem protection

Engineering & Digital Sciences - Maritime innovation

 

🤝 How ECRIN Works

 

ECRIN provides you with powerful tools to:

Declare Future Research - Share your upcoming projects and research interests

Showcase Publications - Upload and share your scientific contributions

Discover Collaborators - Find researchers with complementary expertise

Visualize Opportunities - Access analytical insights on collaboration potential

Connect Globally - Network with 6,000+ researchers across 13 countries

 

🚀 Why Participate?

 

Your participation in ECRIN is crucial for:

Research Excellence - Access to cutting-edge collaborative opportunities

European Integration - Be part of the European University strategy

Career Development - Expand your international research network

Innovation Impact - Contribute to solving global maritime challenges

Resource Optimization - Maximize research funding opportunities

 

⚙️ The process

 

To define your upcoming research projects, ECRIN invites you to select relevant keywords and topics based on the OpenAlex bibliographic database. OpenAlex is a robust, open-source index of scholarly works, authors, institutions, and concepts, continuously updated and widely adopted across the global research community. Relying on a single, standardized knowledge base ensures consistency in topic classification and enables reliable large-scale data analysis. This unified approach allows ECRIN to map research themes, detect potential collaborations, and generate meaningful insights across the institutions.

We strongly invite participants to fully engage with this process by choosing the most accurate and meaningful terms to represent their research. Thoughtful keyword selection not only strengthens the platform's ability to identify potential collaborations, but also increases the reach and clarity of your work within the broader research community.

To further support project definition, free-text fields will also be available to enter a title and a short abstract. These fields are designed to help structure and clarify your submission while complementing the keyword-based analysis.

You are welcome to describe as many upcoming research projects as you wish. After completing your first entry, you'll be invited either to finish or to submit another project - it's entirely up to you! While at least one complete project is required to be included in the matchmaking analysis, the more projects you submit, the greater your chances of connecting with the right collaborators.

 

📊 Expected Results

 

Once the form is completed, it will be used to generate an egocentric graph representing your future research activity. This graph will reflect your profile through the topics (T), methods (M), geographical areas of application (A), and keywords (K) you selected. These elements will then be connected to one or more Common Research Teams (ECRs) within the Campus of Maritime and Port Territories or the EUNICoast.

 

 

 

The combination of all egocentric graphs will generate a community graph. This collective network will support the collaborative analysis of scientific dynamics - helping to describe, understand, and guide emerging research connections. Each contributor will have graphical access to this community graph, enabling them to visually explore the ecosystem and identify potential collaborators.

 

 

Shape the Future of Maritime Research

 

Join thousands of researchers who are already transforming coastal and maritime science through ECRIN. Your expertise, research interests, and publications are the building blocks of tomorrow's breakthrough discoveries.

 

 

 

Your participation matters. Every researcher profile, every shared project, and every publication added to ECRIN strengthens our collective ability to address the pressing challenges facing coastal and maritime communities worldwide.

 

Together, we're not just building a platform - we're building the future of collaborative maritime research.
